MASTER SHARING AGREEMENT (ERSTWHILE USO TOWERS INFRASTRUCTURE)

MASTER SHARING AGREEMENT (ERSTWHILE USO TOWERS INFRASTRUCTURE) ("Agreement") is
executed at New Delhi and is dated as of the/@’day of May, 2014...........

BETWEEN:

KEC International Limited, a Company registered under the Companies Act, 1956 having its registered
office at RPG House, 463, Dr. Annie Besant Road, Worli, Mumbai 400030 and its office at Building
No 9A, 8 Floor, DLF Cyber City, Phase III, Gurgaon (hereinafter referred to as ‘INFRA PROVIDER
(IP), which expression shall unless repugnant to the subject or context mean and include it successors

and permitted assigns)

That, herein after for the reference purpose under this Agreement “INFRA PROVIDER (ZP)” shall also
be referred to as “Infrastructure Provider (ZP)" of the FIRST PART.

AND

BHARAT SANCHAR NIGAM LIMITED a Company incorporated under the Companies Act,1956 having its
registered and corporate office at ‘Bharat Sanchar Bhawan’ H.C. Mathur Lane, Janpath, New Dethi-110001
(hereinafter referred to as “Telecom Service Provider (TSP)" which expression shail unless repugnant to
the subject or context mean and include its successors and permitted assigns) and

That, herein after for the reference purpose under this Agreement “Telecom Service Provider (TSP)"
shall also be referred to as “Telecom Service Providers/Universal Service Provider (TSP/USP)/Service
Provider” of the SECOND PART;

WHEREAS. INFRA PROVIDER (IP), as IP, had created Passive Telecom Infrastructure for 381 specified
locations in remete and rural areas across 4 no: of telecom circles, under an agreement with USO
Administrator, with each tower being shared by three mobile Telecom Service Providers, as universal
service providers ("USPs") as they were then referred to, also selected by USO Administrator.

AND WHEREAS, as per clause i(x} of Section IV of Part A and i(viiil) of Section IV of Part B of their
respective agreements with the Administrator USOF, both IPs and the erstwhile USPs are to enter into
commercial agreement as per mutually agreed terms and conditions for continued operation for providing
mobile services from these towers.

Now, this agreement is applicable for those erstwhile USO Towers for which the IP decides to offer to
the Telecom Service Providers (erstwhile USPs) and the Telecom Service Providers opt to retain / use
the same by way of executing Individual Site Agreement, after the expiry of the ‘subsidy period’ of the
respective tower unless specified otherwise.

The USO Towers, for which the subsidy period has expired before the execution of this Agreement, shall

also be governed by the terms and conditions of this Agreement from the date on which the subsidy

period for the respective USO Tower expired subject to USP opting to retain / continue using those USO
Towers beyond the subsidy period by signing of Individual Site Agreement. }

1. DEFINITIONS, AGREEMENT PARTS, PRIORITY

1.1 DEFINITIONS
In this Agreement, including the Recitals, the following terms have the following meanings:

111 ADMINISTRATOR means the Administrator; Universal Service Obligation Fund in the Department
of Telecommunications under Ministry of Communications & IT.

112 AFFILIATE in relation to a Party means any person which, directly or indirectly, controls such
Party or is controlled by such Party or is under common contro! with such Party; For the purposes of
this Agreement, “Control” means the power and ability to direct the management and policies of the
controlled company through ownership of or control of more than 90 (fifty) percent of the voting
shares of the controlled company, either by contract or otherwise;

113 ANNEXURE/SCHEDULE means the Annexure/Schedule attached to this Agreement at the time of
its execution and/or as may be attached thereafter on mutual consent.

114 BASE TRANSCELVER STATION (BTS): Means a base transceiver station (BTS) which includes/
incorporates the equipment in a network that facilitates wireless communication between user
equipment (UE) like mobile phones, computers etc. and a network.

115 CAPTIONS / HEADINGS: The Captions / Headings of this Agreement / Annexure / Schedules are
inserted for convenience only and are not to be construed as part of this Agreement or the
applicable Service Order or in any way limiting the scope or intent of its provision. The headings or
the captions of the paragraph or chapters should not be used as a guide to interpret the paragraph
or chapter. They shall be for reference only.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF PARTIES
2.1 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) OBLIGATIONS

2.1 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall provide existing Infrastructure Sites including the Passive
Infrastructure and its operation and maintenance services on shared basis to the Telecom Service
Provider as per Schedule i and Schedule 2 of this Agreement.

2.4¢2 INFRA PROVIDER (TP) shail be fully responsible for the watch and Ward, maintenance and
operations of the Infrastructure Site and its Passive Infrastructure as specified in the Service
Level Agreement annexed hereto.

2.1.3 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for proper upkeep of the electric meters and the DG
sets,

a..4 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall undertake maintenance activities of Passive Infrastructure at mutually
agreed planned outage periods,

2.095 ENFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for the statutory compliances and liasioning with
municipal / government bodies in connection with the Infrastructure Sites, Passive Infrastructure
and Services covered under this Agreement.

2.1.6 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for the payment of statutory levies / municipal charges
and monthly consumption charges of power and fuel.

2.17 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall ensure that apprepriate land purchase agreements/lease
agreements/license agreements as required under Applicable Law are in place for the
Enfrastructure Sites during the provisioning of Services, as contemplated here under this
Agreemeni.

2.1.8 Should an additional External Telecom Service Provider be offered an existing Site where Telecom
Service Provider is operating, and such External Telecom Service Provider shall require alteration
of the existing Site, such alteration shall be carried out by INFRA PROVIDER (IP) (including
changing of position of the TE) provided that INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall inform the Telecom
Service Provider about the necessary alterations at least 15 days in advance and shal! coordinate
and cooperate with the Telecom Service Provider so that any such alterations shall not adversely
affect the quality and performance of TE of the Service Provider and these alterations shall be
performed with minimum disturbance of the activities of the Telecom Service Previder on the Site
and at no cost to the Telecom Service Provider, either directly or indirectly,

along with LAT / LONG details are as given in Schedule 4.

bOT / TEC and maintain the same on regular basis.at mutually agreed rates. Any penalty imposed by
boT/TERM, TRALI on USP on account of non-availability of the signages after its fixing and during
the period for which the advance payment for maintenance has been made by the Service Provider,
shall be payable by the Infra Provider and shall be adjusted against IP’s payables. Any other
requirement related to passive infrastructure imposed by the DOT/TEC etc shall be mutually
discussed and action shall be taken as per mutually agreed terms and conditions.

2.1.11 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall provide uninterrupted accesses to all Sites to the TSP. A failure by IP
to provide such access for more than seven (7) days except the cases which falls under the Force
Majeure shall be considered a material breach of this Agreement entitling Telecom Service
Provider to terminate the relevant Site Agreement without liability and without payment of any
Exit Amounts.

2.2 TELECOM SERVICE PROVIDER OBLIGATIONS

2.2.1 The Telecom Service Provider agrees to utilize such Passive Telecom Infrastructure on shared basis
with INFRA PROVIDER (IP) and also with other External Telecom Service Providers of INFRA
PROVIDER (TP).

2.2.2 Subject to other clauses of this Agreement, the Telecom Service Provider shall use the
Infrastructure Site only for the purpose of installation, operation and maintenance of its TE and
use the said Site and its Passive Telecom Infrastructure for telecommunication activities,

2.2.3 The Telecom Service Provider shall carry out the installation of TE at the Sites and shall also
maintain the TE in proper operating condition and maintain the same as to appearance and safety.
The Telecom Service Provider shall ensure that operations of its TE does not interfere, obstruct
or restrict use of the Site by INFRA PROVIDER (IP) or any other user of the Site and do not
cause any harm /nuisance to public in which case both the parties shall mutually work towards
resolution of the same. The Telecom Service Provider shall allow access to its TE at any Sites only
to its designated employees/ agents (“representatives”) and shall communicate the list of such
representatives in respect of each Site to INFRA PROVIDER (IP). The Telecom Service Provider
shall be solely responsible for ali acts and/or omissions of the representatives at the Sites.

2.2.4 During the commissioning of TE and subsequent operations and maintenance of the TE, the Telecom
Service Provider shall ensure that all the safety measures as mandated / required / desirable for
the execution of the job, are properly taken care of by his employees and Telecom Service Provider
shall at all times comply with its HSW Policy, (The copy of HSW policy is attached to this
Agreement as Schedule 8,

2.2.5 The Telecom Service Provider shall at all times comply with Applicable Law relevant to the TE and,
if requested by the IP, shall reasonably assist the IP with requests from any legal or statutory
body.

2.2.6 The Telecom Service Provider shall hold all necessary licenses, permits or other authorizations in
relation to the sharing of Infrastructure Sites and provisioning of the Services.

3. COMMENCEMENT DATE AND FEES
A, COMMENCEMENT DATE
3.Laq FOR EXISTING USPS;

The commencement date shall be the date on which the subsidy period of the tower is over. The
likely commencement date in respect of each tower shall be indicated in the Individual Site
Agreement and is also indicated in the list of towers as given in Schedule 4. The same shall be
jointly agreed/ confirmed between IP and TSP at circle level. However, in case of dispute, the date
of expiry of the subsidy period, as per DoT CCA records, shall survive and shall be jointly agreed by
the Parties within 30 days of execution of this Agreement.

3.2 FOR NEW TELECOM SERVICE PROVIDERS:

Ci} The date which is agreed to between the Parties in an Individual Site Agreement ; or

Gi) The date on which a Site (for which an Individual Site Agreement has been executed)
actually becomes available and ready for use by the Telecom Service Provider: whichever
is later.

3.2 All the towers, whose subsidy period has already expired before execution of this Agreement and
for which the Individual Site Agreement has been executed, shall also be covered under this
Agreement, The commencement date for such towers shall be determined as per clause 3.1.

commencement date was prior to the signing of this agreement shall be signed at one go within 30

6.0 PROCEDURES TO BE FOLLOWED BEFORE INSTALLATION OF EQUIPMENTS

6.1. BY NEW TELECOM SERVICE PROVIDERS OTHER THAN EXISTING USPs:

6.1.1 The Telecom Service Provider shall place a separate Site Request for each Site; INFRA PROVIDER
(IP) shall convey its acceptance to the Telecom Service Provider within 2 weeks of receipt of the
Site Request as a Site Proposal. The Telecom Service Provider shall issue a Service Order within
one week from the date of Site Proposal, which shall be accepted by INFRA PROVIDER (IP) within
one week thereafter. In the event INFRA PROVIDER (IP) desires any changes/amendments in the
Service Order, IP shall communicate the same to Telecom Service Provider within one (1) week of
receipt of the Service Order, after which the Parties shall mutually decide the same within one
week and thereafter the Telecom Service Provider shall issue a revised Service Order to INFRA
PROVIDER (IP). Upon acceptance of the Service Order/revised Service Order as the case may be

’ by INFRA PROVIDER (IP) (within one week), the same shall be treated as a valid Service Order and

will form part of this Agreement. The Site Request, Site Proposal and Service Orders shall be as
per format given in Schedule 7.

threats or intimidation of any description,

3. Workers employed by the Vendor at the sites shall not be required to work extreme hours or work
without adequate rest periods,

4. No child or under age labour shall be used on the cell sites by Vendor. All workers have reached
the age of 15, or have reached the minimum legal working age under national law, whichever age is
the higher.

5. Workers under the age of (8 shall not work at night or under potentially hazardous conditions.
Vendor shall have adequate worker management systems for young workers in place.

6. The cell sites maintained by Vendor shall, at all times, be safe and hygienic with an adequate
number of safe and accessible fire exits from all buildings and workers shall have access to
drinking water.

7. Workers’ life or limb shall not be endangered due to the use of dangerous machinery, unsafe
building structure or layout, or hazardous chemicals. Where serious or fatal accidents have
occurred Vendor shall demonstrate, to the satisfaction of Telecom Service Provider, that all
appropriate steps have been taken to prevent similar accidents occurring in the future.

8. Vendor shall not engage in bribery, corruption or other similar unethical practices in order to gain
competitive advantage.

9, Vendor shall comply with all national health and safety laws with regard to employment of laborers
on the cell sites,
